summary.brief: Povrzenich, L. v. Ripepi, J.. Medical malpractice case involving paediatrics.

evidence_index.text: Lacey was born on January 31, 1996, with a cleft palate for which she underwent several surgeries.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: From 1997 to 2007, Lacey was treated for five urinary tract infections (“UTI”) by her pediatrician, Dr. McCracken.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: In 2008, Lacey changed primary care providers and came under the care of pediatrician Jennifer Ripepi. M.D.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: A urine screen at that time revealed a UTI, as well as high creatinine levels.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Lacey was diagnosed with end-stage renal disease secondary to severe reflux nephropathy.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: In 2015, Lacey underwent a kidney transplant with a kidney donated by her mother.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Judgment affirmed in part and vacated in part. Orders vacated denying post-trial motion for limited new trial and assessing delay damages.
evidence_index.speaker: Judge's conclusion

evidence_index.text: We conclude that the trial court abused its discretion in excluding Nurse Bissontz’s testimony.
evidence_index.speaker: Judge's analysis

evidence_index.text: The jury attributed eighty-five percent of the negligence to Dr. McCracken, and fifteen percent to MVCHS.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Case remanded for a limited new trial as to future medical expenses and reassessment of delay damages.
evidence_index.speaker: Judge's conclusion

initial_state.chief_complaint: Left-sided abdominal pain occurring daily and lasting several hours
initial_state.history_of_present_illness: Lacey presented with complaints of left-sided abdominal pain. She had a history of urinary tract infections and was previously treated with antibiotics.
medications: Antibiotics (specific type not documented in judgment)
initial_state.initial_working_problem: Recurrent urinary tract infections with abdominal pain

requestables.name: Urine Culture
requestables.description: Culture to identify urinary tract infection

reveal.decision_impact: Should prompt further investigation of renal function

requestables.name: CT Scan of Pelvis
requestables.description: Imaging to assess abdominal and pelvic organs

timeline_phases.name: Initial Presentation
timeline_phases.description: Patient presents with abdominal pain and history of UTIs

key_events.event: Urine culture and serum creatinine test

decision_points.clinical_context: Patient with recurrent UTIs and elevated creatinine levels
decision_points.prompt: What should be the next step in management?

options.description: Refer to nephrology for further evaluation
options.clinical_reasoning: Referral to a specialist is warranted due to abnormal renal function tests
Failed to address underlying renal issues

expected_action_court.description: Referral to nephrology should have been made
expected_action_court.standard_of_care_basis: Expert testimony indicated need for specialist evaluation

explanation.why_defendant_wrong: Failure to refer to a specialist led to delayed diagnosis of renal disease
explanation.what_should_have_happened: Referral to nephrology for further evaluation of renal function
explanation.legal_standard_applied: Standard of care as established by expert testimony

patient_outcome.description: Patient developed end-stage renal disease requiring kidney transplant
patient_outcome.long_term_effects: Chronic kidney disease, dialysis, potential need for second transplant

key_findings: Negligence in failing to refer to a specialist and follow up on abnormal labs
Causation established - earlier intervention could have prevented progression to end-stage renal disease

malpractice_determination.point_of_failure: Failure to refer to nephrology and follow up on abnormal labs
malpractice_determination.counterfactual: Specialist referral could have led to earlier diagnosis and treatment

factual_timeline.event: Lacey treated for UTI and high creatinine levels
Lacey diagnosed with end-stage renal disease

allegations.allegation: Failure to refer to nephrology and follow up on abnormal labs

court_findings.legal_significance: Failure to meet standard of care
